channel.title: Posts on Lucaji’s Pages
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Lucaji’s Pages

item.title: ATPiano app - privacy policy
item.description: ATPiano PRIVACY POLICY ATPiano (the “App”) provides this Privacy Policy to help you (the “User (s)”) make an informed decision about whether to use or continue using this software product: the App itself. If you do not agree to these practices, please do not use this software and uninstall from your device(s). As the author is based in Europe, this document conforms to the European Union’s (EU) General Data Protection Regulation (GPDR).

item.title: Lucaji the extended biography
item.description: It was the early ’80s and we kids were totally different from today’s: I used to play on unpaved streets, scratching my knees on the the dirt, dirting and ripping my clothes and building shacks next to swamps. Not many years after, something changed and computers started to made their way through our lives. I have been in touch with electronics and calculators a little bit more than my peers, the ones with the Commodore 64 and a color monitor while I had an Apple//e with green phosphors.

item.title: Cometa Rossa - Area - Demetrio Stratos
item.description: Area with Demetrio Stratos Cometa Rossa Greek Phonetic Italiano Άνοιξε χείλι μοι, ̉άνοιξε, Ánoixe cheíli moi, ánoixe, Aprimi le labbra, aprile γλικά νά τραγουδήσω. gliká ná tragoudíso. dolcemente affinché io canti. ̉Άνοιξε τήν καρδιά. Ánoixe tín kardiá. Apri il cuore. Κομήτη κλει̃̃σε Komíti kleĩse Cometa, chiudi τό στόμα στούς ποιητές. tó stóma stoús poiités. la bocca ai poeti.

item.title: Comunicazione Visiva
item.description: I was enrolled at Istituto Tecnico Industriale Statale “Luigi di Savoia” in Chieti, Italy. That was one of the best technical high schools at the time where I was lucky enough to have some of the best teachers around. Probably I was not the best pupil, but I would love to thank Prof. Giacomo Pisani (physics), Prof. Lucio Di Gregorio (human science) and Prof. Francesco Casale (human science). The following text (still in Italian language) has been recovered with a OCR software and adapted to Markdown.
